summary: Polish the PSA sidebar brand animation, repair dark-mode contrast, and hide cancelled orphan approval-chain nodes.

notes: psa_timesheet_approval_chain now excludes cancelled runtime nodes that no longer belong to the active template graph.
This prevents historical route-refresh or rollback remnants from appearing as current approval-chain nodes.
The migration reloads the PostgREST schema cache after replacing the function.
Replaced the static sidebar PSA square with a restrained black-white-gray animated logo.
The logo animation plays on page entry and can be replayed by hovering, while reduced-motion users see a static PSA mark.
Fixed Tailwind dark variant binding so dark-mode utilities consistently apply under next-themes.
Moved dashboard trend chart colors to explicit theme tokens for readable light and dark mode axes, grid lines, and area fills.
Improved project-name truncation in analysis distribution charts and removed unnecessary project code from the compact list.
Adjusted department pills to use theme-aware foreground text so configured low-saturation department colors remain readable.

verification: Run npm --prefix frontend run build with VITE_APP_VERSION=V0.18.23 and production VITE variables.
Run deploy/scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh on the cloud host with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.23.
Run scripts/smoke-frontend-render.mjs against production with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.23.
known_warnings: Vite may still warn that some chunks are larger than 500 kB.
The PSA logo animation intentionally stays scoped to the sidebar brand square and does not change the wider navigation icon system.
